observation.summary: Provider adapter ACL docs still kept C4 raw-to-draft, backpressure, bridge, instruction placement, and detect helper boundaries as manually edited markdown while adjacent EventIngestor and provider-draft evidence already existed.

hypothesis.summary: An adapter-acl manifest can generate root plus five manual detail pages, link adjacent EventIngestor/provider-draft/search-path evidence, and verify source anchors in bridge, detectutil, session, process, runtimeactor, supervisor, and telemetry instruction code.

execution.summary: Generate the adapter ACL index and detail pages from SSOT fragments while leaving EventIngestor authority under its existing dedicated generator.

evaluation.summary: The verifier rejects generated doc drift, missing source anchors, missing fragments, missing workflow wiring, and missing coverage registration.

retrospective.summary: The C4 adapter ACL docs become generated evidence and reduce the provider-runtime manual hotspot while preserving adjacent evidence ownership.

evidence.proves: provider adapter ACL docs are generated and source anchors still exist
bridge, detect helper, instruction placement, backpressure, and OpenClaw override behavior still match the documented boundary
public CI uploads provider adapter ACL evidence
